data.title: Lululemon Stock Tumbles as Analyst Slams Instructions for Defective Leggings, Saying 'Honestly, That's a Joke'
data.description:
The company that effectively created the athleisure market is currently fighting to save its reputation. Lululemon Athletica (LULU) is seeing its shares tumble as it deals with a massive quality control failure involving its latest high-end yoga pants. The stock hit a new low yesterday, tumbling by 3.24% to close at $180.35. The crisis reached a boiling point last week during a global meeting where Chief Brand and Product Activation Officer Nikki Neuburger addressed hundreds of employees. Instead of a celebration of new designs, the meeting focused on why the $108 "Get Low" tights were revealing far too much when customers moved.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Executives Speed Up the Design Process

The recent failure of the "Get Low" line suggests that Lululemon is prioritizing speed over testing. Under the leadership of Calvin McDonald, the company worked on "reducing its product development process" to get items to stores faster. However, this has led to a string of "self-inflicted wounds." Retail analyst Neil Saunders noted that when a company sells a premium product, it shouldn't have to issue instructions to women on how to wear it because the product is defective. Saunders stated, "honestly, that's a joke," regarding the company's suggestion that customers just need to wear different underwear to make the leggings work.

Board Searches for a New Leader

As the company tries to fix its image, it is also dealing with a leadership vacuum. McDonald is stepping down, and the company is being led by an interim team while they interview for a new chief. Elliott Investment Management is reportedly pushing for a retail veteran like Jane Nielsen to take the helm. Founder Chip Wilson has also stepped up his public criticism, nominating three directors to the board. He believes the company has drifted away from its core fitness enthusiasts.

Loyal customers are feeling the shift too; social worker Amore Prince tried the new leggings in a store but found they didn't work. Even after staff encouraged her to try a different size, Prince said, "this is just one of those items that just doesn't work."

Brand Identity Suffers from "Random" Collaborations

In an effort to find new growth, Lululemon has moved into categories that have confused its long-time fans. The company spent $500 million on the Mirror fitness device, only to discontinue it three years later. It also launched a line of personal care products that failed to gain traction.

Recent partnerships have also missed the mark; a collaboration with Disney was "trashed" by social media users who called it "a random collab" and "basic AF." Simeon Siegel of Guggenheim Securities noted that early adopters likely feel "disenfranchised" as the company moves away from the high-quality basics they originally came to love.

Is Lululemon a Good Stock to Buy?

On TipRanks, Lululemon stock has a Hold consensus rating based on one Buy and 20 Hold ratings. The average 12-month LULU price target of $211.71 implies 17.4% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, LULU shares have fallen 56%.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: CAT Earnings: Caterpillar Stock Climbs on Strong Q4 Results
data.description:
Caterpillar (CAT) stock was up on Thursday following the release of the construction equipment manufacturer's Q4 2024 earnings report. The company reported adjusted earnings per share of $5.16, which was well above Wall Street's estimate of $4.71. The company's adjusted EPS also grew slightly from the $5.14 reported in Q4 2024.

Caterpillar reported revenue of $19.1 billion during its fourth quarter of 2025, which was better than the $17.85 billion that analysts expected. The company's revenue also increased 17.8% year-over-year from $16.21 billion. Caterpillar's revenue growth was powered by:

  • A 15% increase in Construction Industries revenue.
  • A 13% jump in Resource Industries revenue.
  • A 23% boost in Power & Energy revenue.
  • A 1% growth in All Other Segment revenue.
data.author: William White

data.title: Tesla (TSLA) Stock Back in Focus after Neuralink FDA Update
data.description:
Neuralink announced this week that it is ready to implant its Blindsight vision device in a human patient, pending U.S. Food and Drug Administration approval. The company said the device aims to restore vision by sending signals straight to the brain's visual area, without using the eyes or optic nerve.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The update came two years after Neuralink's first brain chip implant in a patient with paralysis. The company said it has now expanded trials to 21 people around the world, up from 12 last fall. Neuralink also confirmed that the FDA granted Blindsight a Breakthrough Device label in September, which can speed up the review process.

Elon Musk said early vision from the device would look like "Atari graphics," with low detail at first. He added that quality could improve over time as the brain learns to read the signals. For now, the device is aimed at people who have lost both eyes and the optic nerve, where no other treatment exists.

From a market view, Neuralink remains a private company with no direct stock exposure. Still, the progress matters to investors because it adds weight to Musk's record of moving high-risk ideas into real-world testing. As a result, that record often feeds into how markets view his public firms.

How This Connects to Tesla and Market Views

Tesla Inc. (TSLA) is the public company most linked to this news. Although Neuralink and Tesla are separate, investors often view Musk's companies as part of a wider tech system. In simple terms, Neuralink supports the idea that Musk-led teams can build advanced systems that link software, hardware, and human use.

This matters for Tesla because its long-term case rests less on car sales alone. Instead, it leans on AI, robotics, and smart systems that work in the real world. Neuralink's progress adds another proof point that Musk's groups can handle complex tech under strict rules.

In addition, Neuralink's trial data may help boost trust in Musk's AI plans more broadly. That trust can affect how investors judge Tesla's future projects, even if the products never overlap.

What a Future Neuralink IPO Could Mean

Looking ahead, Neuralink's steady trial growth also raises talk of a future IPO. While no timing has been shared, the Breakthrough Device status signals a clear path toward approval and later sales. For public market investors, that reduces the sense that Neuralink is only an idea-stage venture.

If Neuralink does go public one day, it could attract both tech and health-focused investors. More importantly, it would place a market value on a business built around human brain links, which few firms can claim today.

Is Tesla Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Let's look at Elon Musk's only publicly traded company – Tesla. The company has a Hold consensus view, based on 26 analysts' ratings. The average TSLA stock price target is $397.79, implying a 7.80% downside from the current price.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: "2026 Will be Massive": Wedbush Analyst Remains Bullish on Apple Stock (AAPL) ahead of Q1 Earnings
data.description:
Apple (AAPL) is set to report its fiscal first‑quarter results on Thursday, January 29, after the bell. Heading into the print, Wedbush analyst Daniel Ives reiterated his Buy rating and $350 price target, arguing that Wall Street is still underestimating what Apple is poised to deliver in 2026.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In a note to clients, Ives said he expects strong holiday‑quarter results, pointing to resilient iPhone demand and a healthier supply chain backdrop. Also, Ives believes 2026 will mark a turning point for Apple as the company finally leans deeper into its long-anticipated AI strategy.

Analyst Sees Big AI-Driven Growth Ahead

According to the Top analyst, this year could be when "Cook & Co. dive into the deep end of the pool on its AI strategic roadmap," setting the stage for a more aggressive push into generative AI across Apple's ecosystem. That includes integrating new AI capabilities into upcoming hardware cycles and expanding software-driven services that could boost margins.

The analyst noted that Apple can shift momentum from the iPhone 17 to the iPhone 18, even as the company faces competitive pressure in China. He expects the company's AI features to play a key role in sustaining demand and refreshing the product pipeline.

Despite macro uncertainty and some weakness in key markets, Ives remains bullish. He kept Apple on both the Wedbush Best Ideas List and the IVES AI 30 List, saying the company is entering a multiyear growth cycle driven by AI, services, and the upcoming iPhone upgrade.

AAPL's Q1 Earnings and Revenue Expected to Grow

The positive rating comes as Apple prepares to report its Q1 results. Investors are watching closely for any hints about Apple's AI roadmap, early demand trends for the latest devices, and how the company is positioning itself amid rising competition.

Currently, analysts expect AAPL to post revenue of $138.39 billion in Q1, up from $124.30 billion in the year-ago quarter. Further, the company is expected to report earnings of $2.68 per share, compared with earnings of $2.40 in the prior-year quarter.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 1/29/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Climb as Big Tech Earnings Loom
data.description:
U.S. stock futures edged higher early Thursday as traders reacted to Big Tech earnings and the Federal Reserve's decision to keep interest rates unchanged. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.36%, 0.25%, and 0.09%, respectively, at 3:47 a.m. EST on January 29.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Wednesday's regular trading, the three major averages closed mixed, with the S&P 500 briefly crossing the 7,000 mark before ending the day down 0.01%, following the Fed's interest rate decision. Meanwhile, the DJIA rose 0.02%, and the Nasdaq Composite edged up 0.17%, boosted by strong performances from Nvidia (NVDA) and Micron Technology (MU).

After the market closed, tech giant Microsoft (MSFT) stock dropped 6.1% due to slower cloud growth in the fiscal second quarter and softer margin guidance for the current quarter. In contrast, Meta Platforms (META) shares popped 7% on issuing a stronger-than-expected sales outlook for the first quarter and increased capex. Tesla (TSLA) stock rose 2.2% after beating Q4FY25 sales and earnings expectations despite reporting its first annual sales decline.

On the economic front, traders will watch data on weekly jobless claims, GDP growth, factory orders, and balance of trade.

Turning to earnings season, major companies reporting today include Apple (AAPL), Lockheed Martin (LMT), Mastercard (MA), Caterpillar (CAT), and Comcast (CMCSA).

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.26%. WTI crude oil futures were trending higher, hovering near $64.17 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price increased to nearly $5,532 per ounce on Thursday, surpassing the $5,500 mark for the first time amid a flight to safety as the U.S. dollar weakened.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened mixed today as traders digested corporate earnings reports.

Asia-Pacific Markets Trade Higher

Asia-Pacific markets traded mostly higher on January 29, after the U.S. Fed's decision to hold rates steady and gold's continued rally.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index gained 0.51%. In China, the Shanghai Composite rose 0.16%, while the Shenzhen Component lost 0.37%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei added 0.03%, and the Topix rose 0.28%.

Stay ahead of macro events with our up-to-the-minute Economic Calendar — filter by impact, country, and more.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Why Is Wall Street Cautious on SoFi Technologies Stock Ahead of Q4 Earnings?
data.description:
Fintech SoFi Technologies (SOFI) is scheduled to announce its results for the fourth quarter of 2025 on January 30. SOFI stock has risen 52% over the past year, driven by the company's impressive growth and continued expansion of its member base. However, shares have pulled back by more than 9% over the past month, as the company's recent capital raise has triggered concerns about shareholder dilution. Moreover, several analysts are worried about SOFI stock's high valuation.

data.title: SK Hynix Stock Gains Attention as AI Triggers 'Craziest Time Ever' Memory Shortage
data.description:
Samsung Electronics (SSNLF) and SK Hynix (HK:7709) warned that the global market for memory chips is tightening fast and may stay that way through 2027. Both companies said supply is being pulled toward AI data centers, leaving fewer chips for phones and PCs.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During its earnings call, SK Hynix said PC and mobile makers are already struggling to get enough supply. "PC and mobile clients are struggling to secure memory supplies," said Joo Seok Min, executive vice president of DRAM marketing at SK Hynix.

At the same time, the core issue is not weak output but limited space to expand. Samsung said cleanroom capacity will remain tight in 2026 and 2027, which limits how much new supply can be added.

Consumers and Device Makers Feel the Pressure

The shift is happening because AI memory used in servers earns much higher returns than standard memory. As a result, chip makers are sending more supply to data centers and less to consumer devices.

According to IDC, data centers may use about 70% of all memory chips made in 2026. TrendForce analyst Avril Wu called the situation "the craziest time ever," while IDC described it as a lasting shift toward AI demand.

Because of this, prices are rising across the supply chain. SK Hynix said some customers are cutting shipment plans or lowering memory specs to manage costs.

The impact is already visible in sales forecasts. Research firms now expect global smartphone shipments to fall at least 2% this year. Meanwhile, the PC market could shrink as much as 8.9% in 2026, after growing 8.1% last year.

Samsung's own mobile unit reported a 10% drop in quarterly profit. Samsung executive Cho Seong jin said 2026 will likely be a hard year, with flat or lower phone shipments due to memory prices.

How Major Companies Are Responding

Apple Inc. (AAPL) is under close watch as costs rise. Supply chain analyst Ming Chi Kuo said Apple plans to absorb higher RAM costs instead of raising prices for the iPhone 18. Apple is also moving to quarterly pricing talks with suppliers, instead of the usual six-month cycle.

Other firms are taking a different path. Lenovo Group Ltd. (LNVGY) said it expects to pass higher memory costs on to buyers. Some PC vendors have warned customers about price increases of 15% to 20%.

Synopsys Inc. (SNPS) CEO Sassine Ghazi said the shortage could last for years. "Most of the memory from the top players is going directly to AI infrastructure," he said, adding that other markets are now starved of supply.

Overall, the message from the industry is clear. As AI demand grows, memory supply is being redirected, and consumer devices are facing higher costs and tighter access that may last longer than many expected.

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to align all stocks mentioned in the text with other smartphone makers and chip manufacturers, which are also affected by the current landscape.
data.author: Ran Melamed
